event: initially evaluated in the Obstetric Emergency Department
blood work showed signs of systemic infection
abdominal ultrasound showed moderate fluid in all quadrants
septic shock with an abdominal source
multiple interloop, retrosplenic, subfrenic, pelvic and retrouterine abscesses
admitted to the Intensive Care Unit
new laparostomy with progressive closure technique
four days after the first intervention
isolated an Escherichia coli and Streptococcus orallis
the patient started amoxicillin with clavulanic acid
kept obstetric follow-up of the remaining uncomplicated pregnancy
admitted for an elective caesarean section
giving birth to a healthy child
to this date the child remains thriving
